articles.title: Artemis II Astronauts Head Home After Historic Journey Around the Moon
articles.summary: The NASA lunar flyby took the four crew members farther from Earth than any humans. They witnessed a solar eclipse and received praise in a call from President Trump.

articles.title: Michigan muscles its way to program's 2nd NCAA basketball title, beating UConn
articles.summary: High-scoring Michigan had to get down and dirty to dig out the national title Monday, making only two 3-pointers all night but still muscling its way to a 69-63 victory over stingy, stubborn UConn.

articles.title: Vance to Visit Hungary to Boost Orban Before Election
articles.summary: A scheduled visit by the American vice president, JD Vance, makes clear that Russia is not the only country invested in a victory for Hungary’s leader, Viktor Orban.

articles.title: Why Hungary’s Election Could Swing on Roma Votes
articles.summary: Prime Minister Viktor Orban’s policies affecting the Roma minority have put those voters in play in upcoming parliamentary elections. In a tight race, they could make the difference.

articles.title: In Paris’s Catacombs, Can a Restoration Breathe New Life Into City’s Dead?
articles.summary: For centuries, the bones of some six million people were buried in the catacombs beneath the city. Curators are trying to preserve and modernize the tunnels while maintaining the spooky ambience.

articles.title: Trump Says Iran Proposal Isn’t Enough to Stop Attacks on Bridges and Power Plants
articles.summary: President Trump has told Iran it must open the Strait of Hormuz by 8 p.m. Tuesday or face the consequences, although he has delayed previous deadlines.

articles.title: Hegseth Likens Easter Rescue of U.S. Airman to Resurrection of Jesus Christ
articles.summary: President Trump also asserted that God supports the American war against Iran “because God is good, and God wants to see people taken care of.”

articles.title: Iran’s 10-Point Proposal Demands an End to Attacks and Sanctions
articles.summary: As President Trump’s deadline for new attacks loomed, Iran conveyed its conditions through Pakistani intermediaries.

articles.title: Trump provides details behind the dramatic rescue of an American airman trapped in Iran
articles.summary: President Trump revealed many of the dramatic details on how the U.S. military scrambled to rescue two members of fighter jet that was shot down deep inside Iran.

articles.title: What can Artemis II astronauts see that satellites haven't captured?
articles.summary: The astronauts on Artemis II will observe parts of the moon rarely seen by human eyes. A NASA planetary scientist said it will offer a vital perspective for lunar research.

articles.title: Gas prices are high. What can you do about it?
articles.summary: With gasoline prices averaging above $4 a gallon nationally, drivers are grappling  with a sharp rise in fuel costs. How can you get the most out of every fill-up?

articles.title: Even when Arsenio Hall's show was a hit, 'everyone wanted it to be something else'
articles.summary: Hall's late-night show gave hip-hop a home on TV and helped propel Bill Clinton to the White House. "I wanted to do this show that didn't exist when I was a kid," he says. Hall's memoir is Arsenio.

articles.title: Medical supplies are stuck in Dubai, as clinics around the world face shortages
articles.summary: The war in Iran has slowed down international shipping, much of which contains medical and humanitarian goods destined for Asia and Africa.

articles.title: Trump reiterates threats to bomb Iran's power plants and bridges
articles.summary: The president has had mixed messages about how and when the U.S.-Israel-led war in Iran will end.

articles.title: Over-the-counter medication abortion? These researchers say it would be safe
articles.summary: A paper in JAMA Internal Medicine adds to the growing scientific evidence that medication abortion pills would be safe to sell over-the-counter at the pharmacy. But political opposition means that pos

articles.title: Supreme Court clears the way for Bannon contempt case to be dismissed
articles.summary: Bannon spent four months in prison after defying a subpoena from the House Select Committee investigating the Jan. 6 attack.

articles.title: Trump threatens Iran's power plants, bridges. And, Artemis II readies for lunar flyby
articles.summary: Trump threatened to bomb Iran's power plants and bridges unless it opens the Strait of Hormuz. And, NASA's Artemis II crew prepares to make its closest approach to the moon.

articles.title: Iran rejects a U.S. ceasefire plan as Trump again threatens to bomb its infrastructure
articles.summary: Iran's top officials pushed back against a U.S. ceasefire plan and President Trump's deadline to open the Strait of Hormuz, striking a defiant tone as the warring sides traded missile attacks.

articles.title: A music festival booked Kanye West, now known as Ye, and lost major sponsors
articles.summary: The rapper Ye was announced as the headliner for the Wireless Festival in London. He's gained notoriety over the years for his antisemitic comments and activities glorifying Nazis.

articles.title: Will Hungary's far-right leader Viktor Orban be voted out of power?
articles.summary: NPR's Rob Schmitz speaks with Zsuzsanna Vegh, program officer at the German Marshall Fund of the United States, about what's at stake in the upcoming election in Hungary.

articles.title: US airman rescued after his fighter jet was shot down in Iran
articles.summary: NPR's Tom Bowman reports on the race to rescue a U.S. airman after his fighter jet was shot down.

articles.title: In Lebanon, more than 50 medics have been killed by Israel. Some say they're targeted
articles.summary: Lebanon says at least 54 health workers are among more than 1,400 people killed by Israel during the current invasion. Human rights groups say first responders are being targeted — something Israel de

articles.title: Dominion still has pending lawsuits against election deniers such as Rudy Giuliani and Sidney Powell
